Mahindra Thar.performance_feel: A lifestyle off-roader that is now more comfortable and practical for daily use. Both 2.0-litre turbo-petrol and 2.2-litre diesel engines are powerful and refined, offering good performance. Ride quality is improved, though still a bit firm. It's a fun car to drive.
Mahindra Thar.driveability: Much more refined and easier to drive on the road compared to its predecessor. Ride quality is improved, making it more comfortable over broken roads, though still a bit firm.
Mahindra Thar.city_performance: More comfortable and practical for daily use, suggesting improved city manageability. Powerful and refined engines offer good performance, likely aiding low-end torque for city traffic.
Mahindra Thar.highway_performance: Powerful and refined engines offer good performance for highway cruising and overtaking. Improved ride quality contributes to better comfort at highway speeds.
Mahindra Thar.off_road: Highly capable off-roader with 4WD, low-range transfer case, mechanical locking differential, and electronic brake locking differential. Ground Clearance: 226 mm, Approach Angle: 41.8 degrees, Departure Angle: 36.8 degrees, Ramp Over Angle: 27 degrees.
Mahindra Thar.crawl: Hill Descent Control and a low-range transfer case (4L)
Mahindra Thar.manual_transmission_performance: The manual gearbox is a 6-speed unit and it feels quite slick to operate. The throws are short and precise, and the clutch is light enough for city driving.
Mahindra Thar.automatic_transmission_performance: The automatic transmission is a 6-speed torque converter unit. It’s smooth and responsive, making city driving a breeze. Kickdown response is decent, and it holds gears well when needed. There are no paddle shifters.
Mahindra Thar.pedal_operation: Clutch is light enough for city driving.
Mahindra Thar.gear_shift: Manual gearbox feels quite slick to operate. The throws are short and precise.
Mahindra Thar.gear_selection: Gear throws are short and precise.
Mahindra Thar.pedal_travel: Brake pedal travel is a bit long.
Mahindra Thar.ride: The ride quality is on the stiffer side, especially at lower speeds. It tends to jostle occupants over broken patches and potholes. However, as speeds increase, the ride settles down and feels more composed on highways.
Mahindra Thar.ride_quality: The ride quality is on the stiffer side, especially at lower speeds. It tends to jostle occupants over broken patches and potholes. However, as speeds increase, the ride settles down and feels more composed on highways.
Mahindra Thar.stiff_on_pot_holes: Tends to jostle occupants over broken patches and potholes.
Mahindra Thar.bumps: Smaller bumps and undulations are felt inside the cabin more prominently. Larger bumps are absorbed reasonably well, but the rebound can be a bit harsh.
Mahindra Thar.shocks: Suspension setup is on the stiffer side, tuned for off-road performance. Rebound can be a bit harsh for larger bumps.
Mahindra Thar.nvh: Improved significantly in terms of NVH levels compared to its predecessor. However, it’s still a rugged SUV, and some engine noise and road noise do filter into the cabin, especially at higher speeds.
Mahindra Thar.powertrain_nvh: Some engine noise and drivetrain noise filter into the cabin, especially at higher speeds. The diesel engine is a bit clattery at idle, but it smooths out once on the move. The petrol engine is more refined.
Mahindra Thar.wind_nvh: Noticeable above 100 kmph, particularly from the soft-top variants. The hard-top offers better insulation.
Mahindra Thar.road_nvh: Road noise filters into the cabin, especially at higher speeds.
Mahindra Thar.wind_noise: Noticeable above 100 kmph, particularly from the soft-top variants. The hard-top offers better insulation.
Mahindra Thar.tire_noise: Present, especially with the all-terrain tyres, but it’s not overly intrusive.
Mahindra Thar.shakes: A bit of body-on-chassis wobble at high speeds, but nothing worth complaining about.
Mahindra Thar.steering: Light at city speeds, weighs up nicely at higher speeds, offers decent feedback
Mahindra Thar.manoeuvring: Large turning radius makes U-turns a task, parking in tight spots requires effort
Mahindra Thar.stability: Stable at triple-digit speeds, but noticeable body roll around corners.
Mahindra Thar.straight_ahead_stability: Stable at triple-digit speeds on the highway.
Mahindra Thar.braking: Decent stopping power, pedal feel could be better. ABS and EBD standard. Front: Disc, Rear: Drum.
Mahindra Thar.brakes: Front: Disc, Rear: Drum, ABS with EBD
Mahindra Thar.brake_performance: Decent stopping power (expert assessment), no specific 100 kmph distance provided.
Mahindra Thar.airbag_types_breakdown: Driver and Passenger airbags (2 airbags total).
Mahindra Thar.vehicle_safety_features: ABS with EBD, Electronic Stability Program (ESP) with Roll-over Mitigation, Hill Hold Control, Hill Descent Control, ISOFIX Child Seat Mounts, Tyre Pressure Monitoring System (TPMS)
Mahindra Thar.impact: Adult Occupant Protection: 12.52/17 (4 Star), Child Occupant Protection: 41.11/49 (4 Star)
Mahindra Thar.seats_restraint: 3-point seatbelts (front and rear), Pretensioners & Force Limiter Seatbelts, ISOFIX Child Seat Mounts, Seat Belt Warning.
Mahindra Thar.interior: Dual Tone Dashboard. Qualitative assessment of materials, fit-and-finish, soft-touch surfaces, premium feel: Not found.
Mahindra Thar.climate_control: Automatic Climate Control, Heater. AC type (single-zone/dual-zone): Not found. Rear vents: Not found.
Mahindra Thar.seats: Adjustable Head-rests (front), Rear Seat Headrest, Height Adjustable Driver Seat. Seat comfort, bolstering, cushioning, long-drive comfort assessment: Not found.
Mahindra Thar.seat_cushion: Adjustable lumbar support is no longer available on the front seats.
Mahindra Thar.seat_material: All-black leatherette upholstery with suede inserts (Thar Roxx Star Edn).
Mahindra Thar.seat_features_detailed: Driver seat: Height Adjustable. 6-way/8-way power, lumbar support, memory, ventilation, heating: Not found.
Mahindra Thar.rear_seat_features: Rear Seat Headrest, Rear Seat Centre Arm Rest. 60:40 split fold, recline angle, cup holders, rear AC vents: Not found.
Mahindra Thar.soft_trims: Dashboard will use soft-touch materials (Thar Roxx).
Mahindra Thar.armrest: Rear Seat Centre Arm Rest available. Front armrest: Not found. Quality, padding, height: Not found.
Mahindra Thar.infotainment_screen: 7-inch (17.8 cm) Touchscreen Infotainment System with BlueSense App Connectivity
Mahindra Thar.digital_display: Multi-Information Display (MID); specific size and type (TFT/LCD) not specified
Mahindra Thar.apple_carplay: Apple CarPlay and Android Auto (wired, not specified as wireless)
Mahindra Thar.button: AC temperature control and mode buttons on the central console seem to be waterproof.
Mahindra Thar.audio_system: Roof Mounted Speakers (brand and count not specified)
Mahindra Thar.parking: Rear Parking Sensors, Hill Assist, Hill Descent Control
Mahindra Thar.chasis: Redeveloped version of the ladder frame chassis
Mahindra Thar.engine: mHawk 130 CRDe, D117 CRDe, 2.0L mStallion 150 TGDi
Mahindra Thar.front_suspension: Independent Double Wishbone with Coil Over Damper & Stabiliser Bar
Mahindra Thar.rear_suspension: Multi-Link Coil Spring Suspension with Stabiliser Bar
Mahindra Thar.full_led: LED headlamps with daytime running lights
Mahindra Thar.boot_organizer: Luggage net hooks in boot space
Mahindra Thar.crawl_smart: An off-road cruise control system that can be switched on between 2.5 and 30 kph
Mahindra Thar.intelli_turn: Can be activated under 15 kph to make tight turns by locking the rear inner wheel

Hyundai Creta.performance_feel: The Creta offers a comfortable and refined driving experience. The 1.5-litre turbo-petrol engine is particularly impressive, offering strong acceleration and a sporty feel. Handles predictably and safely.
Hyundai Creta.driveability: Engines are smooth and responsive, making it easy to navigate through city traffic. Light steering and good visibility further aid in urban driving.
Hyundai Creta.torque: 144 Nm @ 4500 rpm (1.5L MPi Petrol), 250 Nm @ 1500-2750 rpm (1.5L U2 CRDi Diesel), 253 Nm @ 1500-3500 rpm (1.5L Turbo GDi Petrol)
Hyundai Creta.response: Engines are smooth and responsive. The diesel engine provides excellent low-end torque.
Hyundai Creta.city_performance: Engines are smooth and responsive, making it easy to navigate through city traffic. The light steering and good visibility further aid in urban driving. Diesel engine provides excellent low-end torque.
Hyundai Creta.highway_performance: On highways, the Creta feels stable and composed, even at higher speeds. Overtaking is effortless, especially with the more powerful engine options.
Hyundai Creta.off_road: Ground Clearance: 190 mm. Not equipped with 4WD/AWD.
Hyundai Creta.manual_transmission_performance: The 6-speed manual gearbox offers smooth shifts and a light clutch, making it easy to operate in city traffic.
Hyundai Creta.automatic_transmission_performance: The IVT (CVT) with the 1.5-litre petrol is smooth and convenient for city use. The 6-speed torque converter with the diesel is also smooth and responsive. The 7-speed DCT with the turbo-petrol is quick and offers a sporty driving experience, with paddle shifters for manual control.
Hyundai Creta.pedal_operation: Light clutch, easy to operate in city traffic.
Hyundai Creta.ride_quality: Suspension setup is well-tuned to absorb most undulations, providing a smooth experience for occupants over city bumps and remaining stable and composed on highways.
Hyundai Creta.stiff_on_pot_holes: Well-tuned suspension absorbs most undulations over broken roads and city bumps, providing a smooth experience.
Hyundai Creta.bumps: Suspension setup is well-tuned to absorb most undulations.
Hyundai Creta.shocks: The Creta’s suspension setup is on the softer side, which helps it glide over most bumps and undulations with ease. However, at higher speeds, the ride can feel a bit floaty, and there’s noticeable body roll around corners.
Hyundai Creta.powertrain_nvh: The 1.5-litre naturally aspirated petrol engine is smooth and refined. The 1.5-litre diesel engine can be a bit noisy on cold starts, but settles down once warm.
Hyundai Creta.tire_noise: Minimal road and wind noise entering the cabin; NVH levels are well controlled.
Hyundai Creta.blower_noise: AC blower can get a bit noisy at higher speeds.
Hyundai Creta.jerks: The 7-speed DCT offers quick and smooth shifts. Manual gearbox options are also smooth.
Hyundai Creta.grabby: Brakes are strong and offer good progression.
Hyundai Creta.steering: Steering is light at low speeds, making it easy to manoeuvre in the city, and weighs up nicely as speeds increase, offering good confidence on highways.
Hyundai Creta.manoeuvring: Easy to manoeuvre in the city and tight spaces due to light steering and compact dimensions.
Hyundai Creta.stability: The 2024 Hyundai Creta facelift offers good high-speed stability and feels planted on the highway. Body roll is predictable and well-controlled for an SUV, and the suspension provides a comfortable ride while absorbing bumps.
Hyundai Creta.corner_stability: The Creta’s suspension is tuned for comfort, which means it soaks up bumps well. However, this also means there is a fair bit of body roll when cornering at speed.
Hyundai Creta.straight_ahead_stability: At highway speeds, the Creta feels stable and planted, inspiring confidence.
Hyundai Creta.braking: The braking performance is adequate, with a progressive pedal feel. The all-wheel disc brakes (available on higher variants) offer good stopping power.
Hyundai Creta.brakes: Front Disc, Rear Disc (SX(O) variant), ABS, EBD. Brake Assist not explicitly mentioned.
Hyundai Creta.brake_performance: Braking performance is good, with strong stopping power and a progressive pedal feel. The car stops in a straight line, and all-wheel disc brakes (on higher variants) contribute to confidence. No specific 100-0 kmph braking distance is widely reported in reviews.
Hyundai Creta.airbag_types_breakdown: 6 airbags: Driver, Passenger, Front Side, Curtain. Rear Side and Knee airbags are not present.
Hyundai Creta.vehicle_safety_features: ABS, EBD, ESC, VSM, HAC, ISOFIX
Hyundai Creta.adas: Forward Collision Warning & Avoidance Assist, Blind-Spot Collision Warning & Avoidance Assist, Lane Keeping Assist, Lane Departure Warning, Driver Attention Warning, Safe Exit Warning, Smart Cruise Control with Stop & Go, Lane Following Assist, High Beam Assist, Leading Vehicle Departure Alert, Rear Cross-Traffic Collision Warning & Avoidance Assist
Hyundai Creta.impact: 5-star Bharat NCAP rating. Adult Occupant Protection (AOP) score of 29.64 out of 32 points (92.63%). Child Occupant Protection (COP) score of 44.71 out of 49 points (91.24%).
Hyundai Creta.seats_restraint: Height Adjustable Front Seat Belts, Pretensioners & Force Limiter Seatbelts, 3 Point Seatbelt in Middle Rear Seat are present.
Hyundai Creta.interior: The interior of the Creta is well-appointed, with a mix of soft-touch materials and hard plastics. The fit and finish are good, and the overall design is modern and ergonomic. Higher variants get leatherette upholstery and a panoramic sunroof, adding to the premium feel.
Hyundai Creta.climate_control: Automatic Climate Control with Rear AC Vents.
Hyundai Creta.seats: Front seats are well-cushioned, supportive, and offer good bolstering, making them comfortable for long drives. The driver's seat features 8-way power adjustment and ventilation. Rear seats provide good space, comfort, and decent thigh support, suitable for two adults on long journeys, with a third passenger potentially feeling snug.
Hyundai Creta.seat_cushion: Front seats feature good cushioning, lateral support, and under-thigh support. Rear seats also offer good under-thigh support, contributing to overall comfort for long journeys. Specific cushion density is not mentioned.
Hyundai Creta.seat_features_detailed: Driver seat: Manual height adjust, Lumbar Support, Front Ventilated Seats. Power adjustment, memory, and heating not found.
Hyundai Creta.rear_seat_features: Rear seat: 60:40 Split Rear Seat, Rear Seat Centre Arm Rest, Front & Rear Cup Holders, Rear AC Vents. Recline angle not found.
Hyundai Creta.ventilated_seats: Front only, available in SX(O) and SX Tech variants
Hyundai Creta.visibility: All-round visibility from the driver's seat is generally good, aided by an elevated driving position, reasonably slim A-pillars, and large glass areas. Rear visibility is decent, further assisted by a large rear window, parking sensors, and a reverse camera. The ORVMs are well-sized, minimizing blind spots.
Hyundai Creta.soft_trims: The interior features soft-touch materials on the upper section of the dashboard and soft-touch inserts on the door pads, enhancing the premium feel. Lower sections of the cabin still utilize hard plastics, but they are well-finished.
Hyundai Creta.armrest: Front Centre Armrest, Rear Seat Centre Arm Rest. Quality, padding, and height details not found.
Hyundai Creta.headrest: Adjustable Headrests (Front & Rear). Comfort for tall passengers not found.
Hyundai Creta.egress: Entry and exit from the Hyundai Creta are easy due to wide-opening doors and an optimal seat height that is neither too low nor too high. The sill height is manageable, and there is ample headroom, making it convenient for most occupants.
Hyundai Creta.ingress: Easy to get in and out of thanks to its ideal ride height and wide-opening doors.
Hyundai Creta.resolution: Crisp graphics on the 10.25-inch touchscreen infotainment system.
Hyundai Creta.digital_display: 10.25-inch (26.03 cm) Multi-display Digital Cluster. Type and specific displayed information not detailed.
Hyundai Creta.button: Physical buttons and knobs for climate control and other functions feel premium and offer good tactile feedback.
Hyundai Creta.parking_camera: 360-degree Surround View Monitor (SVM) with Rear Parking Camera and dynamic guidelines, displayed on 10.25-inch infotainment screen
Hyundai Creta.tyre_size: 215/60 R17 (SX, SX(O) variants), 205/65 R16 (E, EX, S, S(O) variants)
Hyundai Creta.orvm: Electrically adjustable, auto-fold, integrated turn indicator
Hyundai Creta.window: All 4 power windows, one-touch up/down on driver window, auto-up with pinch guard
Hyundai Creta.wiper_control: Rain Sensing Wiper: Yes, Rear Wiper: Yes
Hyundai Creta.parking: Parking Sensors: Rear, 360 View Camera: Yes, Hill Assist: Yes
Hyundai Creta.door_effort: The doors feel reasonably substantial and close with a reassuring thud, not a tinny clang, indicating good build quality. The effort to close is minimal, and door sealing appears effective in isolating cabin noise.
Hyundai Creta.sensitivity: The steering is light at low speeds for easy city driving and weighs up decently as speeds increase, offering a predictable feel, though not overly communicative. Throttle response is linear, smooth, and well-judged, avoiding sudden jerks. The brake pedal feel is progressive and confident, not grabby or overly sensitive.
Hyundai Creta.engine_displacement: 1497 cc (1.5L MPi Petrol), 1493 cc (1.5L U2 CRDi Diesel), 1482 cc (1.5L Turbo GDi Petrol)
Hyundai Creta.engine: 1.5L MPi Petrol, 1.5L U2 CRDi Diesel, 1.5L Turbo GDi Petrol
Hyundai Creta.max_power_kw: 1.5L MPi Petrol: 84.57 kW, 1.5L U2 CRDi Diesel: 84.57 kW, 1.5L Turbo GDi Petrol: 117.68 kW
Hyundai Creta.full_led: LED headlamps, LED DRLs, and LED tail lamps
Hyundai Creta.console_switches: Physical buttons for climate control, drive mode selector, electronic parking brake, auto hold (in higher variants)
Hyundai Creta.luggage_rack: Roof Rails (Luggage rack is an accessory)
Hyundai Creta.dashcam_provision: Dual Camera Dashcam with Driving & Parking Modes

Power & Torque: Hyundai Creta's 1.5L Turbo GDi Petrol engine delivers 117.68 kW (~158 bhp) maximum power, which is 8 bhp (5.3%) higher than Mahindra Thar's top petrol engine (150 bhp).
Drive Mode: Hyundai Creta utilizes a Monocoque chassis for enhanced on-road dynamics and comfort, differing from Mahindra Thar's rugged Ladder frame chassis.
Exterior: Hyundai Creta features Tri-Beam LED Headlamps with Automatic Headlamp Leveling and Auto High Beam, while Mahindra Thar uses Halogen Headlamps.
Hyundai Creta offers an Electric Panoramic Sunroof, which is not available in Mahindra Thar per its official specifications.
Hyundai Creta has Electrically adjustable, auto-folding ORVMs with integrated turn indicators, while Mahindra Thar's ORVMs are electrically adjustable only.
Hyundai Creta includes Rain Sensing Wipers and Auto Defogging, features not explicitly mentioned for Mahindra Thar.
Capabilities: Hyundai Creta has a tighter turning radius of 5.2 m, making it more maneuverable than Mahindra Thar's 5.7 m.
Hyundai Creta offers superior mileage: 17.4-18.4 kmpl (Petrol) and 19.1-21.8 kmpl (Diesel) compared to Mahindra Thar's 15.2 kmpl.
Hyundai Creta provides a larger boot space of 433 litres, whereas Mahindra Thar has 300 litres.
Hyundai Creta is lighter with a kerb weight of 1200-1300 kg, compared to Mahindra Thar's 1750 kg.
ADAS: Hyundai Creta features a comprehensive Level 2 ADAS suite including Forward Collision Warning & Avoidance Assist, Lane Keeping Assist, Lane Departure Warning, Driver Attention Warning, Smart Cruise Control with Stop & Go, Lane Following Assist, Rear Cross-Traffic Collision Avoidance Assist, and High Beam Assist, a more extensive list of specific features than Mahindra Thar's generic 'ADAS Level 2 features' and 'Blind Spot Monitoring'.
Interior: Hyundai Creta offers a 5-seater configuration, while Mahindra Thar is a 4-seater.

Hyundai Creta features a larger 10.25-inch HD Touchscreen Infotainment System and a 10.25-inch Multi-display Digital Cluster, compared to Mahindra Thar's 7-inch touchscreen and Multi-Information Display (MID).
Hyundai Creta comes with a premium Bose 8-speaker Sound System, whereas Mahindra Thar has 4 roof-mounted speakers.
Hyundai Creta provides dual-zone temperature control for its Automatic Climate Control, while Mahindra Thar has single-zone.
Hyundai Creta offers power windows for all four doors with one-touch up/down on the driver's side and anti-clamping, contrasting with Mahindra Thar's power windows only for the front.
Hyundai Creta includes an 8-way power adjustable driver seat with lumbar support, while Mahindra Thar only has a height-adjustable driver seat.

Hyundai Creta features a Cooled Glove Box, Welcome Lighting, Ambient Lighting, auto-dimming IRVM, soft-touch IP dashboard, and sunvisors with vanity mirrors, which are not present or mentioned for Mahindra Thar.
Hyundai Creta offers USB Type-C ports in both front and rear rows; Mahindra Thar only provides a 12V socket.
Hyundai Creta has front and rear cup holders, while Mahindra Thar only has front.
Hyundai Creta includes a Dual Camera Dashcam, which Mahindra Thar does not.
BRAKES: Hyundai Creta (in higher variants) is equipped with All-Wheel Disc Brakes (Front & Rear Disc), while Mahindra Thar features Front Disc and Rear Drum brakes.
Hyundai Creta includes an Electronic Parking Brake (EPB) with Auto Hold, which is not available in Mahindra Thar.
Others: Hyundai Creta comes with 6 airbags as standard, compared to Mahindra Thar's 2 airbags.
Hyundai Creta has achieved a 5-star Bharat NCAP rating, superior to Mahindra Thar's 4-star Global NCAP rating.
Hyundai Creta features crash sensors, power door locks, and a panic alarm.

Exterior & Interior: Mahindra Thar is a 3-door SUV (2 front, 0 rear doors), offering a distinct rugged aesthetic, unlike the 5-door Hyundai Creta.
Engine: Mahindra Thar's 2.0L Turbo-petrol engine offers up to 320 Nm of torque, which is 67 Nm (26.5%) higher than Hyundai Creta's most powerful 1.5L Turbo GDi petrol engine (253 Nm).
Mahindra Thar's 2.2L Diesel engine offers 300 Nm of torque, which is 50 Nm (20%) higher than Hyundai Creta's 1.5L Diesel engine (250 Nm).
Drive Mode & Capabilities: Mahindra Thar is a true 4-Wheel Drive (4WD) vehicle, featuring a low-range transfer case, mechanical locking differential, and electronic brake locking differential for superior off-road performance. Hyundai Creta is a Front-Wheel Drive (FWD) SUV without these specialized off-road components.
Mahindra Thar boasts significantly higher Ground Clearance at 226 mm, which is 36 mm (18.9%) more than Hyundai Creta's 190 mm.
Mahindra Thar includes an Intelli-Turn system (activated under 15 kph to make tight turns by locking the rear inner wheel), a unique off-road feature not found in Hyundai Creta.
Mahindra Thar features a dedicated Off-Road Info Display, absent in Hyundai Creta.
Mahindra Thar has a rugged Ladder Frame Chassis, purpose-built for extreme conditions, unlike Hyundai Creta's monocoque construction.

Mahindra Thar offers specific terrain modes (Snow, Sand, Mud) alongside a 'crawl' feature with Hill Descent Control and low-range transfer case. Hyundai Creta has general 'Terrain Modes' but lacks the detailed off-road hardware.
ADAS: Mahindra Thar explicitly mentions a PAB (Passenger Airbag) Deactivation Switch, which is not specified for Hyundai Creta.
Others: Mahindra Thar offers a Dolby Atmos audio system, which is not explicitly mentioned for Hyundai Creta.
Mahindra Thar has a stiffer suspension setup, optimized for off-road durability, resulting in a firmer ride quality at lower speeds compared to Hyundai Creta's comfort-tuned suspension.
Mahindra Thar explicitly mentions a 'Warning Triangle Tail Door'.
